{{Quote|I am victim, a refugee, yes. I flee… fled from my world once the Sith fleet comes. They bomb my world, destroy it, yes?|translated=1|Kni, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Kni''' was a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. During the war, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombed]] Kni's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. He was one of the few who escaped the destruction, and by [[3956 BBY]], Kni had taken up residence at the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|But I run here, find the Jedi already here, unknown. They take me in… one left of a billion, yes…|translated=1|Kni, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:TheEbonHawkDockingWithTheJediEnclave.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Following the destruction of his homeworld, Kni found himself living at the Jedi Enclave on Dantooine.]]
Kni was a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. During the conflict, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombed]] Kni's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The attack decimated the landmasses and [[Temperature/Legends|boiled]] the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of the world, [[Death/Legends|killing]] the population on its surface. Kni fled when the [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith fleet]] arrived and narrowly survived the attack, becoming a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]]. He eventually found his way to the [[Farm/Legends|agrarian]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], reaching a secret [[Jedi Enclave]] where the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] allowed him to take up residency.<ref name="KOTOR" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', which includes the appearance of Kni, in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> he was approached in the Enclave's courtyard by former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who had [[Amnesia/Legends|lost]] his memories and was [[Jedi training/Legends|retraining]] as a Jedi. While talking about his own past as a refugee, Kni recognized Revan as a Jedi and thanked him and the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Enclave's Council]] for allowing him to stay. They spoke about the ongoing war, with Kni explaining how he felt about both sides in the conflict.<ref name="KOTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Ah, yes, you are a Jedi. I am grateful that your people harbor me in my times of need.|translated=1|Kni, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
Kni was a Duros male who had [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and teal skin. He was grateful to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for allowing him the reside at the Enclave. Kni was capable of understanding [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], but he spoke in [[Durese/Legends|Durese]] with peculiar grammar, ending many statements as if they were questions and appending "yes" to many of them.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|After my flight, all I have left to give is my thanks, which your Order gladly has.|translated=1|Kni, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
While living on Dantooine, Kni wore a white-and-mauve [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]], mauve pants, green-and-white [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]]. Due to his [[Time/Legends|time]] spent fleeing, he had very few material possessions.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kni appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. Interactions with him are optional and have no affect on the plot, quests, or the player's alignment.<ref name="KOTOR" />
